title: CSS Box Model
content:
### CSS Box Model

Every HTML element is a **box** with four layers.

---

### The Four Layers

```
+---------------------------+
|        MARGIN             |
|  +---------------------+  |
|  |      BORDER         |  |
|  |  +---------------+  |  |
|  |  |   PADDING     |  |  |
|  |  |  +---------+  |  |  |
|  |  |  | CONTENT |  |  |  |
|  |  |  +---------+  |  |  |
|  |  +---------------+  |  |
|  +---------------------+  |
+---------------------------+
```

---

### Layer Breakdown

| Layer | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Content** | The actual text or image |
| **Padding** | Space between content and border |
| **Border** | Line around padding |
| **Margin** | Space outside border (transparent) |

---

### Total Width Calculation

With default `box-sizing: content-box`:

```css
/* Element with: */
width: 300px;
padding: 20px;
border: 5px solid black;
margin: 10px;

/* Total width = 300 + 20*2 + 5*2 + 10*2 = 370px */
/* Content = 300px */
```

---

### Box Sizing (Recommended)

```css
* {
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
```

With `border-box`, padding and border are **included** in the width:

```css
/* Same element with border-box: */
width: 300px;
padding: 20px;
border: 5px solid black;

/* Content = 300 - 40 - 10 = 250px */
/* Total width = 300px */
```

---

### Inspecting in DevTools

- Open browser DevTools (F12)
- Click the **Computed** tab
- See the full box model visualization
- Blue = content, Green = padding, Yellow = border, Orange = margin

> **Key Point:** Always use `box-sizing: border-box` globally. It makes sizing predictable.
codeExample:
Content area
Width: 250px (300 - 40 padding - 10 border)

Total box width: 300px (including padding and border)

quiz.question: What does box-sizing: border-box include in the width?
